programmes.show_description: The Nursery: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver. Jack and Stan think that Olive is the ideal candidate to become supervisor of the depot's nursery.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
The Busmen's Ball: Sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal. It is the annual Busmen's Ball and the lads decide that they want something a little more exciting this year...
Slave of Duty: Drama series about a Cornish detective. The team are intrigued by the case of a dead man found buried with a pile of 100-year-old wood.
Total Loss: Drama series about a Cornish detective. When a fishing boat sinks killing one of the crew, Kersey and Lane discover a community struggling to keep its head above water.
Wild Justice: When Lewis investigates the poisoning of a visiting female bishop, two more deaths mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy convolute proceedings.
Overture: When DC Endeavour Morse investigates the disappearance of a missing Oxford schoolgirl, he soon risks all in a search for justice that will ultimately shape his destiny.
Happily Ever After: Comedy-drama series about a doctor who moves to a small Cornish town. Martin and Louisa's wedding day arrives, but the day is plagued by all sorts of disasters.
The Italian Rapscallion: Drama series about sleuthing gardeners. A mysterious death occurs while Rosemary and Laura are preparing the garden of a restaurant that is opening in Italy.
Swords into Ploughshares: Light-hearted series about a pair of sleuthing horticulturalists. An archaeologist who posed as Rosemary to gain access to an estate is found dead.
Five Little Pigs: Another case for the Belgian detective. Poirot is called in to re-examine the murder of Amyas Crale, a painter whose wife was hanged for the crime 14 years before.
The Murder at the Vicarage: Colonel Protheroe is intensely disliked, but when he is found slumped over the desk in a vicarage study, Miss Marple must unpick the truth.
Sauce for the Goose: Barnaby and Scott delve into the murder of a visitor to a relish factory and discover a web of secrets and lies within the family that own it.
Blood Will Tell: DCI Vera Stanhope investigates the case of a murdered entrepreneur whose legacy of unpaid debts and ruined lives leaves no shortage of suspects.
Captivating mystery thriller. Jo must find an accomplice to the two dead men when she learns that the three of them targeted a nearby chemical company belonging to Alan Benson.
Captivating mystery thriller. Jo and Sam find themselves with two surprising suspects in custody, and the pressure mounts on Mackie when a discovery turns everything on its head.
Stan's Room: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver. When Stan finds that living at home is cramping his love life, he decides to move out and rent a room.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Viva Espana: Classic British sitcom. When Ringo wins a holiday in Spain, Simon and Oliver are soon vying to accompany him.
Canteen Trouble: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Blakey discovers that Stan and Jack are biting the hand that feeds them.
Greg is annoyed to discover Mel has visited Arthur. Miles becomes jealous when he sees Jake and Jasmine getting along at the Woolpack, and Nicola spies an opportunity at work.
Jake begins his trial run at the Woolpack, where he and Jasmine start to play pranks on each other. Nicola finds more interest in her services, and Ashley suggests a family picnic.
After spending the night in a prison cell, David comes clean about his past misdeeds, but Gail refuses to testify against him. Becky takes one of the builders back to Roy's place.
Gail intervenes during the court proceedings as David calmly accepts his fate and insists he is guilty on all counts. Roy reaches a compromise with Becky over her promiscuity.
Close Encounters: When a security guard is killed at a quarry, Frost discovers that the only witness is a young man who claims that the culprits were aliens from a distant planet.
Rumours: Drama set in the North York Moors. Nick gets an anonymous tip-off about a pop star's undesirable predilections, and Greengrass is in trouble because of village gossip.
Playing with Fire: Drama set in North Yorkshire. Burning desires of all kinds bring problems for Nick and Kate, and a wandering horse and wandering salmon also cause frustration.
The Fisher King: Murder mystery drama. Long-suppressed family secrets, stolen ancient artefacts, and a 30-year-old death at a Celtic burial site result in a series of murders.
Jasmine ends her relationship with Miles as he continues to be jealous of Jake. Nicola impresses David and Viv is furious when her solicitor tells her to plead guilty.
Nicola enlists David to help with a ferocious Alsatian, but they find themselves locked in the B and B. Louise suggests that she and Jamie buy the shop and cafe.
A terrified Jason gets rid of the bats for Tony before the council arrive. The magistrate adjourns David's case for sentencing and Becky finds herself back in Roy's bad books.
Nowt But A Prank: Drama series set in the 1960s about a London policeman who moves to Yorkshire. Nick is held at gunpoint when a feud between neighbours turns into a siege.
Old, New, Borrowed and Blue: Drama. A burglar who tidies up houses, a wedding guest who sells 'black bombers', and a saintly yet suspicious church organist keep Nick on his toes.
Midsomer Rhapsody: Barnaby and Scott investigate a web of family feuds and long-lost love affairs when an inspirational retired music teacher is battered to death with a torch.
Game: Crime drama. Throwing himself into work to mask the heartache of Joan leaving, a young Endeavour Morse is quickly consumed by his duties when a research scientist is drowned.
Close Encounters: When a security guard is killed at a quarry, Frost discovers that the only witness is a young man who claims that the culprits were aliens from a distant planet.
